product_name: ArthroFLEX Decellularized Dermal Allograft (Bio-Implant for Soft-Tissue Repair)
key_features: >=97% DNA removal (<10 ng dsDNA/mg wet weight)
biohospitable collagen scaffold for host cellular and vascular ingrowth
retains growth factors, elastin, matrikines, cytokines and collagens
advantages: RTC repair with human dermal allograft ECM increased ultimate load to failure by 29% and decreased gap formation by 21% compared with non-augmented controls (Ely et al.)
Dermal allograft augmentation increased load to failure by 62% (560 N vs 346 N) in a cadaver rotator cuff model (Omae et al.)
Augmentation can reduce re-tear rates and improve clinical outcomes
In large rotator cuff tears (>3 cm), postoperative healing rates increased to 85% with dermal augmentation vs 40% with standard repair (Barber et al.)
Residual DNA reduced by >97% (approx. 16 ng/mg dry weight), lower than GRAFTJACKET (134.45) and AlloDerm (272.80)
Provides high ultimate load and suture retention strength
Demonstrated ability to remodel and integrate with host tissue after implantation
Resists infection in a rat model better than synthetic polyester mesh
disadvantages: Allografts are expensive and may not be available for all surgical centers (stated for the SCR-with-RCR technique)
clinical_references: Petri M, Warth RJ, Horan MP, Greenspoon JA, Millet PJ. Outcomes after open revision of massive rotator cuff tears with biologic patch augmentation. Arthroscopy. 2016;32(9):1752-1760.

Gilot GJ, Alvarez-Pinzon AM, Barcksdale L, Westerdahl D, Krill M, Peck E. Outcomes of large to massive rotator cuff tears repaired with and without extracellular matrix augmentation: a prospective comparative study. Arthroscopy. 2015;31(8):1459-1465.
Hirahara AM, Andersen WJ, Panero AJ. Superior capsular reconstruction: clinical outcomes after minimum 2-year follow-up. Am J Orthop. 2017;46(6):266-278.
Ely EE, Figueroa NM, Gilot GJ. Biomechanical analysis of rotator cuff repairs with extracellular matrix graft augmentation. Orthopedics. 2014;37(9):608-614.

Conroy C, Sethi P, Macken C, et al. Augmentation of distal biceps repair with an acellular dermal graft restores native biomechanical properties in a tendon-deficient model. Am J Sports Med. 2017;45(9):2028-2033.
Omae H, Steinmann S, Zhao C, et al. Biomechanical effect of rotator cuff augmentation with an acellular dermal matrix graft: a cadaver study. Clin Biomech. 2012;27(8):789-792.

Barber F, Burns J, Deutsch A, Labbe M, Litchfield RB. A prospective, randomized evaluation of acellular human dermal matrix augmentation for arthroscopic rotator cuff repair. Arthroscopy. 2012;28(1):8-15.

Smith MJ, Bozynski CC, Kuroki K, Cook CR, Stoker AM, Cook JL. Comparison of biologic scaffolds for augmentation of partial rotator cuff tears in a canine model. J Shoulder Elbow Surg. 2020;29(8):1573-1583.

product_name: Biceps Tenodesis SwiveLock System (SwiveLock Tenodesis Screw)
key_features: Piloted Headed Reamers with built-in guide tips
advantages: designed for all-arthroscopic proximal biceps tenodesis
designed to save steps and minimize the length of the procedure
steers the biceps tendon to the bottom of the bone socket without the need to externalize or whipstitch the tendon
no need to predetermine tendon length; position and tension are easily set and visualized prior to final anchor insertion
Piloted Headed Reamers eliminate the need to ream over a guide pin
onlay 4.75 mm SwiveLock technique has equivalent biomechanical (load-to-failure) strength to an inlay interference screw
clinical_references: Cagle PJ Jr, London DA, Gluck MJ, Morel S, Parsons BO. Long head of biceps tenodesis at the superior aspect of the biceps groove: A biomechanical comparison of inlay and onlay techniques. Shoulder Elbow. 2020;12(1):12-17.
Scheibel M, Schroder RJ, Chen J, Bartsch M. Arthroscopic soft-tissue tenodesis vs bony fixation anchor tenodesis of the long head of the biceps tendon. Am J Sports Med. 2011;39(5):1046-1052.

Chiang FL, Hong CK, Chang CH, Lin CL, Jou IM, Su WR. Biomechanical comparison of all-suture anchor fixation and interference screw technique for subpectoral biceps tenodesis. Arthroscopy. 2016;32(7):1247-1252.

product_name: Corkscrew (Corkscrew FT / Knotless Corkscrew / Bio-Corkscrew FT)
advantages: superior pull-out strength and less chance for implant micromotion and potential loosening, compared to earlier generation anchors that do not have a continuous thread running the entire length of the implant
torque-to-failure up to three times higher than traditional female hex head driver designs
when inserting into hard bone, the internal drive mechanism substantially increases resistance to stripping
knotless medial pulley provides higher ultimate load than knotted medial pulley (P=.006)
abrasion resistance of the 4.5 mm Bio-Corkscrew FT eyelet has a mean of 8193 cycles
superior load-to-failure and increased torque resistance with significantly less cyclic displacement vs Mitek Healix BR
suture eyelets are superior to traditional polymer or metal eyelets with respect to reduced chance of abrasion and failure
clinical_references: Arthrex, Inc. LA1-00066-EN_A. Naples, FL; 2017.

key_features: two polyester suture implants connected with continuous 2-0 FiberWire suture
advantages: Scientific literature has demonstrated that rotator cuff augmentation with acellular dermal graft can improve healing and reduce retear rates
ArthroFlex demonstrated decreased retear rates and improved patient-reported outcome scores after 1 and 2 years
Biomechanical testing has shown that ArthroFlex dermal allograft provides high ultimate load and suture retention strength
Matracell technology is validated to remove >=97% of donor DNA and cellular content
PushLock anchor allows visualizing and adjusting suture tension prior to anchor insertion to prevent overtensioning of the graft construct
clinical_references: Ely EE, Figueroa NM, Gilot GJ. Biomechanical analysis of rotator cuff repairs with extracellular matrix graft augmentation. Orthopedics. 2014;37(9):608-614.

product_name: FiberTak Soft Anchor (all-suture family: 1.8/2.6 FiberTak, 2.6 FiberTak RC, FiberTak DR, FiberTak Biceps)
key_features: self-punching inserters eliminate the need to predrill or prepunch a bone socket
curved and straight guide delivery systems
2.6 FiberTak RC: fixed 1.7 mm FiberTape with optional tensionable knotless mechanism or sliding 1.3 mm SutureTape
advantages: 52 lb of secure, low-profile knotless suture fixation (Knotless 1.8 FiberTak)
no risk of knot impingement or loosening
1.8 mm drill minimizes bone removal
small 2.6 FiberTak allows multiple points of fixation for increased tissue-to-bone compression
optimized anchor sheath secures with over 70 lb of pull-out strength
1.3 mm SutureTape is 21% more resistant to tissue pull-through than standard FiberWire
repair sutures individually passed, tensioned, and controlled under direct visualization

key_features: proximal laser line for insertion depth
advantages: Knotless techniques minimize the number of surgical steps and save time
Designed specifically for glenoid labral repair to maximize the preservation of glenoid bone
No risk of knot impingement
Cannulated design minimizes anchor volume
Suture tension is visualized and adjusted prior to anchor insertion
Suture-first technique allows suture to be passed independently through a desired amount of tissue prior to anchor implantation
Self-punching PushLock anchors do not require instrumentation

product_name: SpeedBridge (SpeedBridge Knotless Rotator Cuff Repair; includes FiberTak SpeedBridge variant)
advantages: Contact area of the tendon-to-footprint interface increased 14% compared to traditional hard-bodied anchors (FiberTak SpeedBridge)
FiberTak SpeedBridge contact force higher than classic SpeedBridge: 25% increase at 20 N, 26% at 30 N, 30% at 40 N
Biomechanical repair strength comparable to traditional SpeedBridge repair
Increased repair construct strength (482 N) vs #2 suture knot-tying repairs
200% more footprint compression and 30% higher tissue pull-through resistance with FiberTape suture
Procedural time savings of 28% compared to knot-tying repairs
Less likely to result in full-thickness rotator cuff retears vs knot-tying repairs
Knotless low-profile construct without risk of knot impingement
